column1: Generated: 2026-05-18 | Owner: Korakot + Nick | Status: DRAFT — support time line needs filling in
== MODEL A — "Lite as Pro" (each customer: own Railway service + own Postgres DB) ==

column4: Why / Basis
Own Node.js service per customer on Railway Pro. Widget-only traffic is light — mostly booking queries and takeaway menu fetches. Usage-based billing: small idle service ~$2.50, busy service ~$6.
Dedicated Postgres instance per customer. Small DB footprint: bookings + menu cache + daily orders. Estimate $2.50–6/mo per instance on Railway.
$20/mo fixed plan includes $20 of usage credit — essentially the first $20 of usage is free. Fixed cost amortised across all deployments; negligible per customer at any meaningful scale.
Free tier covers static hosting. Widget JS served from Netlify CDN — no cost regardless of customer count.
Free tier: 300 emails/day. Booking confirmations comfortably within limit per restaurant.
DNS CNAME record — no cost. Covered by existing Namecheap domain.
UK card rate: 1.5% + £0.20 per charge. On £39: £0.79. On £49: £0.94. On £69: £1.24. Range £0.79–1.50 across plans.
Infra is cheap. Human support time is the real unknown and can dwarf infra. Estimate: hrs per customer per month × your effective hourly rate. Only Korakot can fill this in.

column1: == MODEL B — Multi-tenant (all customers share one Railway service + one Postgres DB, row-level isolation via restaurant_id) ==

column4: Why / Basis
One shared Node service for all Lite customers. Cost amortised across customer count. At 10 customers sharing a $5/mo service = $0.50/customer (~£0.40).
One shared DB with restaurant_id on every table. Very low per-customer footprint — a few hundred rows per restaurant per day.
Same as Model A — fixed plan cost negligible at scale.
Same as Model A — free tiers unchanged.
Same as Model A — per-transaction cost is identical regardless of architecture.
⚠️ Hidden cost. Retrofitting all ~40 endpoints with tenant middleware = 2–4 weeks of dev time. Amortised over 24 months across projected customers. Higher at low customer count.

⚠️ Cross-tenant data leak = regulatory liability. ICO fine up to 4% of annual turnover or £17.5M whichever is higher. Model A eliminates this risk entirely. Model B requires perfect query discipline forever.
Same unknown as Model A.
Model A infra HIGH (£)

column11: Healthy margin. Still cheaper than ResDiary (~£50/mo) and OpenTable (per-cover fees). Value prop: 24/7 online bookings, zero per-cover charge.
Strong margin. 0% commission saves restaurant £280+/mo vs Just Eat. At £49 this is still a 5.7× ROI for the customer. Recommended range was £49–59 — this is the floor.
£39+£49=£88 separately. Bundle at £69 = £19 saving. Premium anchor — positions bundle as the serious tier, not a discount product. Exceptional margin on both models.

column1: 1. All figures in GBP. Railway costs converted at $1 = £0.80 (approximate — check current rate).
2. Model A margin figures are gross margin before support time, admin, and Korakot's time. Add those to get true net.
3. Model B infra savings vs Model A are real (£3–7/customer/mo) but are partially offset by Phase 2b dev cost and ongoing GDPR risk.
4. Stripe fee in margin table uses the low end (£0.64 on £29, £0.79 on £39, £0.94 on £49) — margin LOW column is therefore conservative.
5. Sources: Railway pricing docs (docs.railway.com/pricing), Stripe UK fees (stripe.com/resources/more/uk-card-processing-fees-101).
